steps.action: Run test_int4_gemv_optimize.py V2 fused (optimized with ubfe + pow2 shift)
steps.expected: max abs error < 1e-2 for N=4096,K=4096 and N=11008,K=4096

steps.action: Verify __builtin_amdgcn_ubfe present in v2 source (from test_m2_decode.py)

evidence.output: V2 fused: max_err=0.000482 [PASS] for all shapes. ubfe present in v3: True, in v2: True. VAL-INT4-001 confirmed.

steps.expected: v3_t16 or v2_fused faster than original v2
steps.observed: v3_t16 @ 29.3us vs v2_fused @ 35.9us for N=4096,K=4096. v3_t16 is 1.22x faster than v2_fused. Significant improvement over original split-K+memset+convert (3 launches).

evidence.output: N=4096 K=4096: WINNER gemv_int4_v3_t16 @ 29.3 us (vs v2_fused 35.9 us). N=11008 K=4096: WINNER gemv_int4_v3_t16 @ 63.7 us (vs v2_fused 63.8 us — parity)

steps.action: Benchmark v3 variants vs v2 in test_int4_gemv_optimize.py
steps.expected: v3 benchmarked and winner wired into engine
steps.observed: v3_t16 is fastest (29.3us vs 35.9us for v2_fused). Engine wired: Uses gemv_int4_v3 (for non-residual): True, Uses gemv_int4_v2_fused (for residual): True
PASS: v3_t16 wired for non-residual GEMV, v2_fused for residual GEMV (VAL-INT4-003)

evidence.output: BENCHMARK SUMMARY: N=4096 K=4096: best=gemv_int4_v3_t16 @ 29.3us, v2_fused=35.9us. Engine: v3 initialized in _init_gemv_v2: True. PASS.

assertions.title: DPP wave reduction in INT4 GEMV

steps.expected: DPP reduction produces correct output max err < 1e-2
steps.observed: gemv_int4_v3_dpp: max_err=0.000482 [PASS] for N=4096,K=4096; max_err=0.000484 [PASS] for N=11008,K=4096

evidence.output: V3 variants test: gemv_int4_v3_dpp: max_err=0.000482 [PASS], grid=(1024,1,1) for N=4096; max_err=0.000484 [PASS], grid=(2752,1,1) for N=11008

summary: Tested 4 assertions: 4 passed, 0 failed. INT4 GEMV uses ubfe/bitfield extract patterns, v3_t16 cooperative-reduction is wired as default (1.22x faster than v2_fused), DPP reduction variant produces correct output.
